cross_service_analysis.note: Note: IdentityCenter internally uses sso-admin boto3 client methods. This is expected and NOT a cross-service issue.
cross_service_analysis.explanation: IdentityCenter rules may use sso-admin or iam methods, which is correct as IdentityCenter integrates with these services. The SERVICE_TO_BOTO3_CLIENT mapping correctly maps identitycenter to sso-admin.
cross_service_analysis.recommendation: No action needed - rules correctly use appropriate API methods for IdentityCenter

rule_fixes.issue: Was using describe_permission_sets (plural) but discovery is describe_permission_set (singular)
Was using describe_permission_sets (plural) but discovery is describe_permission_set (singular)
Had contradictory checks - PasswordLastUsed both exists and not_equals null (same check)
rule_fixes.fix: Removed redundant not_equals check, kept only exists check

known_issues.description: Rules contain hardcoded ARN values that should be dynamic or removed
known_issues.note: These may be intentional test values or need to be replaced with proper logic

known_issues.description: Rules check PermissionSetArn exists which is redundant since it's always present in the discovery

list_permission_sets and list_permission_sets_provisioned_to_account return PermissionSets as array of ARN strings. The emit structure correctly uses items_for, but InstanceArn preservation from parent context may need code-level support when for_each and items_for are both used.
known_issues.note: Current YAML structure is correct; code may need enhancement to fully support this pattern
